name: Komering

sources.author: Ager, Simon
sources.title: Komering language, alphabet and pronunciation
sources.notes: Latin alphabet chart with IPA per letter, and the note that e, f, q, v and x occur only in loanwords and foreign names.

notes: Komering (Kumoring) — Lampungic (Malayo-Polynesian) language of South Sumatra, Indonesia, written today in an Indonesian-based Latin alphabet (the traditional Lampung/Kaganga abugida is out of scope). The map commits to that Latin alphabet as tabulated by Omniglot, cross-checked against the phoneme inventory in the Wikipedia phonology. Indonesian conventions hold: ⟨c⟩ = /tʃ/, ⟨j⟩ = /dʒ/, ⟨ng⟩ = /ŋ/, ⟨ny⟩ = /ɲ/, ⟨y⟩ = /j/. The phonemic vowels are /i u o a/; the letters ⟨e f q v x z⟩ occur essentially only in loanwords and foreign names (⟨e⟩ = [ə]~[e], ⟨f⟩ = [f]~[p], ⟨q⟩ = [q]~[k], ⟨v⟩ = [v]~[b], ⟨x⟩ = [z]~[ks]), and /z/ likewise enters only through loans. The glottal stop /ʔ/ is phonemic but has no dedicated letter in ordinary spelling, so it is not mapped. INPUT CONTRACT: Latin-script Komering in Indonesian-based spelling.

ancestors.notes: Austronesian — the classification node this language sits under.
